rsa definition key rsa definition key rsa definition key rsa definition key rsa definition key RSA, which stands for Rivest-Shamir-Adleman, is a widely used public-key cryptographic algorithm. It was one of the first practical methods for public-key cryptography and is still widely used today for secure data transmission. In RSA, the security of the encryption relies on the difficulty of factoring large composite numbers, typically the product of two large prime numbers. The algorithm involves the generation of a public and a private key. The public key is used for encrypting messages, while the private key is used for decrypting them. This ensures that only the intended recipient, who possesses the private key, can decrypt the message.
